article_title: Rice University Develops MagnetoARPES Technique That Reveals Time-Reversal Symmetry Breaking in a Kagome Superconductor

content_preview.title: Rice University Develops MagnetoARPES Technique That Reveals Time-Reversal Symmetry Breaking in a Kagome Superconductor
content_preview.summary: Rice University physicists built magnetoARPES, a new instrument that revealed the first direct momentum-space evidence of time-reversal symmetry breaking in the kagome superconductor CsV3Sb5, confirming theoretically predicted loop current orders.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Researchers at Rice University have developed a new experimental technique called magnetoARPES that has produced the first direct momentum-space evidence of time-reversal symmetry breaking in a kagome superconductor. The team applied the instrument to the compound cesium vanadium antimony (CsV3Sb5) and detected electronic signatures consistent with loop current orders, a long-theorized but previously unobserved phenomenon in which electrons circulate in opposing directions on a crys...
